extra.reasoning:
rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, whose condition is the full 12-flag conjunction (prior_gi_bleed and pregnancy and renal_impairment and polypharmacy and elderly and qt_prolongation and hepatic_impairment and asthma and bradycardia and diabetes and immunosuppressed and g6pd_deficiency). No other rule touches rec_drug_a, so rec_drug_a = true iff that conjunction holds; otherwise default makes it false.

rec_drug_b is affected by r1 (priority 50, sets true on prior_gi_bleed) and r2 (priority 90, sets false on the exact same 12-flag conjunction as r4). Whenever the full conjunction holds — i.e., exactly the only case in which rec_drug_a becomes true — r2 fires at priority 90 and beats r1 (priority 50), forcing rec_drug_b := false. There is no tie (90 vs 50) and no ordering ambiguity. r2's antecedent is identical to and therefore exactly covers r4's antecedent.

Thus in every state where rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b is forced false by the higher-priority r2; and when the conjunction is false, rec_drug_a is false. The pair (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) can never be jointly true. The mutual-exclusion property holds over the entire 2^14 input space.
